cover.title: AI made it easy to ship more
cover.sub: Google's 2024 DORA report, the largest study of software delivery we have, found that's the problem.

slides.title: +25% adoption, −7.2% stability
slides.body: Rising AI adoption tracked with a 7.2% drop in delivery stability and a 1.5% drop in throughput. ~76% of teams were already using AI.

slides.title: the cause isn't bad AI code
slides.body: It's batch size. AI removes the friction that kept change sets small, and big batches break delivery. DORA has shown this for a decade.

slides.title: more code, less stable delivery
slides.body: You didn't get faster delivery. You got bigger batches. The same pattern METR and GitClear found from different angles.

slides.title: the fix is delivery discipline
slides.body: Small changes, review gates, observability. The unglamorous parts DORA always pointed at, now non-optional at AI volume.

cta.title: find your weakest lever
cta.sub: A scoped read of your agent setup in your real repo, sources and method included. The prioritized plan is yours to keep.
